question: What is the current gap between internal proxy metrics and external outcome validation?
expect: External grounding ratio still ~5%, unchanged since S445
actual: External grounding ratio 4.9% generous / 0.0% strict. Structurally unchanged from S445 baseline (5.04%). Zero resolved external outcomes in 509 sessions.
diff: Expectation CONFIRMED. No meaningful change in 64 sessions (S445-S509). The gap between internal proxy metrics and external outcome validation remains total.

method.external_definition: Cross-swarm citations, academic validation, third-party benchmarks, real-world outcome measurement. Human signals about swarm direction are NOT external. External theory citations used within internal analysis are NOT external validation of swarm output.

signals_external_generous_breakdown.PRED_0001_to_0008_market_predictions: 8 predictions registered S499-S500, ALL status=OPEN, 0 resolved, 0 outcomes measured. First resolve window: PRED-0003 TLT by 2026-04-21

phil16_status.sessions_of_noncompliance_with_S190_crit: 319+ sessions (S190 required 1 external signal per 10 sessions)
phil16_status.ground_truth: Good and helpful beyond itself ASPIRATIONAL. 0 external beneficiaries in 509 sessions. S500 challenge: even internal output has 1.02x human_benefit_ratio (near-zero net benefit).
phil16_status.S506_soul_extraction: human_benefit_ratio=1.02x. 15.4% GOOD, 15.1% BAD, 69.5% NEUTRAL. Meta domain NET NEGATIVE (34 good, 52 bad).

key_events_since_S445: S459: L-1118 structural diagnosis — L-601 loop closure is binding, not dissipation rate
S476: F-GND1 OPENED — grounding enforcement as structural pressure
S499-S500: 8 market predictions registered (PRED-0001..0008) — first external output TARGETS but 0 resolved
S499: PHIL-4 revised — external output now co-equal with self-improvement (was test bed)
S499: math_tree.py built as external tool — 0 external users
fcomp1_progress.first_resolve_window: PRED-0003 TLT by 2026-04-21 (28 days from now)
fcomp1_progress.assessment: F-COMP1 has moved from pure aspiration to registered-but-unresolved predictions. This is structural progress (predictions exist, infrastructure exists) but 0 actual external validation has occurred. The test is whether any PRED resolves correctly AND that outcome feeds back into swarm self-assessment.

comparison_to_S445.delta: Generous ratio: -0.14pp (5.04% -> 4.9%). Strict ratio: -5.04pp (5.04% -> 0.0%). The S445 count of 6 external signals likely used the generous definition (counting contacts/attempts). Under the same generous definition, the ratio is essentially unchanged at 4.9%.
comparison_to_S445.structural_change: PHIL-4 revised to co-equal dual product. F-GND1 opened with enforcement tools. 8 market predictions registered. But 0 resolved external outcomes — the structural plumbing exists but has not yet produced measured results.

why_unchanged: L-601 structural enforcement theorem: voluntary protocols decay to structural floor. External grounding was aspirational since S190 (319 sessions ago). No creation-time enforcement exists that requires external validation before knowledge is accepted.
Orient.py generates tasks from internal state (frontiers, periodics, maintenance). No step checks for or produces external interaction — the execution loop is structurally closed.
The 8 predictions (S499) are the first structural attempt to break out. But registering predictions is not validating them — the system must wait for real-world outcomes (first window: 2026-04-21).

Human signals about swarm direction (80+ entries) are NOT external validation. They are internal direction from a participant node. The swarm has no mechanism to receive validation from entities that are not part of the swarm.
what_would_change_it: PRED-0003 TLT resolving (2026-04-21) and the outcome being honestly compared to prediction
External user testing math_tree.py and reporting results
Cross-swarm citation: another swarm repo citing or using this swarm's outputs
Publishing a paper and receiving peer review (F-SUB1/F-PUB1)
A third-party benchmark where swarm methods are tested against independent baselines

f_gnd1_progress.corpus_grounding_rate: 5% -> 14% (but this measures whether lessons cite external theorists, NOT whether external parties validated swarm outputs — a crucial distinction)
f_gnd1_progress.assessment: F-GND1 improved INTERNAL references to external knowledge (citing theorists in lessons). This is NOT the same as external validation of swarm output. The ratio measures 'do we cite others' not 'do others validate us'.

lesson_candidate.summary: External grounding ratio 4.9% at S509, unchanged from 5.04% at S445 (delta -0.14pp over 64 sessions). Zero resolved external outcomes in 509 sessions. F-COMP1 progressed structurally (8 predictions registered, PHIL-4 revised, grounding tools built) but produced zero measured external results. F-GND1 improved citation-of-externals (5%->14%) which is distinct from validation-by-externals (still 0%). The system has built plumbing for external validation but no water has flowed through it. First test: PRED-0003 TLT resolution by 2026-04-21.
lesson_candidate.key_insight: Registering predictions is not validating them. Building tools is not using them externally. Citing external theorists is not receiving external validation. The swarm has confused preparation-for-grounding with actual grounding. The 64-session unchanged ratio proves that structural preparation without execution produces zero external grounding improvement.
